prompt: I need to understand what Python's itertools module offers. Can you go to docs.python.org and find the itertools page, then give me a summary of its main function groups?

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract_or_map
assertions.description: The agent ran nimble extract or nimble map targeting docs.python.org

assertions.name: covers_main_groups
assertions.description: The output mentions at least 2 of: infinite iterators, combinatoric iterators, chain, islice, groupby, product, combinations, permutations

assertions.name: structured_output
assertions.description: The output is organized into categories or groups, not just a flat list

assertions.name: no_hallucination_check
assertions.description: The output doesn't describe functions that don't exist in itertools (e.g. itertools.map, itertools.filter)
